_doc: Risk-tiering rules read by scripts/wo-risk-classify.sh (C7). Data-driven (OCP): add globs/extensions without touching the kernel. Globs are matched with bash [[ $file == $glob ]] where * spans '/'. A realized-diff file matching any security_glob => tier high (AR-C). A changed file whose extension is in executable_extensions => tier >= medium with a guaranteed security lens. NOTE: 'security in gate_floor' is NOT a trigger (it is invariantly true in the base set, AR-C); the gate_floor signal is 'gate_floor superset of base', computed by the kernel, not here.

tier_model_map._doc: R-3 builder model routing — consumed by ③ lifecycle_controls (work-order-loop) to pick the BUILD ATOM's model from a PRE-BUILD tier estimate (wo-risk-classify.sh run on the WO's declared '## Files to touch'). BUDGET governance / cost lever ONLY: critics and the gate floor ALWAYS run top-model regardless, so an under-estimate degrades cost-efficiency, never the safety floor. Additive data (OCP) — no kernel change. ④ may override.

model_weight_map._doc: ④ safety_governor — model→weight units consumed by scripts/governor.sh to compute the Weighted Dispatch Count (WDC). weight(tier) = model_weight_map[ tier_model_map[tier] ]. Fail-strict: an unknown model maps to _default (2, the opus-equivalent) — undercounting spend is the governor's fail-open (D2), so the default is the HIGHER weight, never 1. Additive data (OCP, MED-1 fix) — re-weighting needs no kernel edit.
